description_long: This town hall building features a long, rectangular structure with a series of distinct roof segments, each with a slightly different color and texture, suggesting a modular or multi-sectioned design. The facade is characterized by repetitive vertical window slits and a prominent, slightly protruding section at one end, which may serve as the main entrance. The color palette includes shades of gray, black, and brown, with the entrance area highlighted by a lighter, tan hue. The roof sections are flat and stepped, with some areas appearing to have additional architectural detailing or textural variation. The building's massing is low and elongated, giving it a modern, institutional appearance. There are no visible decorative elements such as columns or sculptures, and the overall design is functional and minimalistic.
description: A long, low-profile town hall with segmented flat roofs, vertical window slits, and a minimalistic facade in gray, black, and brown tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the building, segmented rooflines, and the prominent entrance section on the right.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the main entrance area, with vertical window slits and a tan-colored facade section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the building's length, stepped roof segments, and a less prominent end section.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back roof segment and the uniform, windowed facade without an entrance.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Minimal ornamentation; repetitive vertical window slits and subtle textural variations on roof segments.
attribute_entrance_design: Single main entryway with a protruding facade section, likely double doors, framed by a tan-colored segment and vertical window slits.
